title: Fat Fresh Norwegian Sloeberries Papillote
description: Rich and juicy Norwegian dish featuring sloeberries, cooked to perfection in a flavorful papillote. Perfect for a lazy Sunday.
instructions: Rinse the sloeberries and pat them dry with a paper towel.
In a small saucepan, melt the butter over medium heat.
Add the brown sugar, lemon juice, salt, and black pepper to the saucepan.
Bring the mixture to a simmer and cook for 2-3 minutes, or until the sugar has dissolved.
Place a sprig of fresh mint on each piece of paper.
Spoon the sloeberries over the mint, leaving a small border around the edges.
Brush the melted butter mixture evenly over the sloeberries.
Fold the paper over the filling to form a triangle or a square shape.
Fold in the edges to seal the papillote and bake for 20-25 minutes, or until the fruit is tender.
Serve warm, garnished with additional fresh mint leaves if desired.
